mechanism_narrative: A low or falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so declines in policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive on average after FEDFUNDS declines, or if the sign flips and rate cuts are followed by negative returns in more than 60% of cut observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above the recent labor-market trend signals deteriorating household income expectations and weaker risk appetite, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-threshold episodes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the conditional average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales reflect stronger consumption demand and revenue growth for cyclicals, so positive RSAFS momentum should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 1-month change turns positive after a negative 3-month streak
failure_condition: Reject if positive retail-sales momentum does not coincide with above-zero 5-day forward returns, or if the signal delivers a hit rate below 52% over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while HOUST remains weak, industrial activity is improving without broad housing-led overheating, implying healthier real activity and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint INDPRO-up/HOUST-soft configuration fails to produce positive 60-day returns on average, or if either proxy alone explains the effect equally well without the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in GDPC1 and PAYEMS indicates broadening real demand and labor income growth, which should lift earnings expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined GDP-and-payroll improvement does not outperform either series alone in predicting 60-day returns, or if triggered episodes have non-positive average forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS rate in the presence of elevated CPIAUCSL reflects restrictive real financing conditions and valuation compression, so the signal should be negative for S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate/high-inflation observations are not followed by negative 20-day returns, or if the interaction term is insignificant while the single series effects remain strong.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle labor slowdown, a rising unemployment rate alongside soft payroll growth signals earnings risk and credit stress, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the UNRATE-up/PAYEMS-down configuration does not produce negative 5-day returns, or if the pattern is no worse than random after conditioning on the regime.

mechanism_narrative: A weak housing start series combined with slowing industrial production signals broad cyclical deceleration that typically weakens earnings revisions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if housing weakness plus industrial slowdown does not precede negative 60-day returns, or if the conditional average return is not below zero in at least 60% of triggered cases.
